body: I'm Po-Chun, an incoming master's student at Carnegie Mellon University, majoring in Electrical and Computer Engineering. My interests include algorithms, consumer-facing applications, computer architecture, electronic design automation, and programming in general. In my undergraduate studies, I have done research on [[/research#formal|solving graph problems using formal verification methods]] and [[/research#analog|analog and mixed-signal IC design]].

**I'm an Entrepreneur.** I see opportunities, enlighten others with my vision, and execute them passionately. I founded the [[/startupleadership/#ntuloc|NTU Learning Optimization Club]] in my sophomore year, and the Chinese Chess Association in high school. I initiated a one-year-long project to create [[/my_projects/#vmks|an online replica of the NTUEE Makerspace]]. I also founded [[/my_projects/#ntupair|NTU Pair]] and [[/my_projects/#synality|Synality]], two networking app startups, and served as a team leader in the [[/startupleadership/#ntucep|creative and entrepreneurial program]].

**I'm a Leader.** I value cooperation, teamwork, and culture. I planned and executed over 25 lectures and activities throughout my one year of service in the [[/startupleadership/#ntueesaad|student association]]. Additionally, I mentored my freshmen groupmates, equipping them with the skills necessary to excel in their future leadership roles. I've also led a team of 20 people to teach high-school students maker skills in the [[/startupleadership/#ntueecamp|NTUEE summer camp]].

**I'm a Maker.** I see problems, use my knowledge as a toolbox, and solve them. For hardware, I designed and built a programmable [[/my_projects#lightbike|glowing bike]], the props for the [[/my_projects#lightdance|NTUEE Light Dance]], and an [[/my_projects#makentu2024|AR device]] for motorists to navigate around the city, for which we won the best maker award in the annual MakeNTU competition. For software, I created a [[/my_projects#ntufood|random restaurant selector]] for NTU students, some [[/projects|JavaScript solitaire games]], and an [[/my_projects#sleksystem|online hospital simulation system]] for SLEK, a medical student organization.
